title: 10 best herbal tinctures for feverish feeling
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for feverish feeling are liquid extracts made from plants, herbs, and botanicals that have been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms associated with fevers. These natural remedies can be taken sublingually or added to water to provide relief from body aches, headaches, and discomfort. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with feverish feeling include peppermint, eucalyptus, and ginger, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease congestion. By using these natural remedies, people can feel more comfortable and restored while their bodies work to recover from illness, allowing them to get back to their daily routines with renewed energy and vitality.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow jessamine tinctures helps with feverish feeling because it contains compounds that have a natural cooling effect on the body, which can help to reduce inflammation and bring down temperatures. The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties also help to alleviate symptoms such as headaches, sore throats, and body aches associated with fever. Additionally, yellow jessamine's antiviral and antibacterial properties help to combat underlying infections that may be causing the fever, promoting a faster recovery from illness.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic Properties: Yellow Jessamine tincture contains compounds that have been shown to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms associated with fever, such as sweating, chills, and headache.

Sedative and Anti-Anxiety Properties: The tincture has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress that often accompanies feverish feelings, promoting relaxation and restful sleep.

Antioxidant and Immune-Modulating Effects: Yellow Jessamine contains bioactive compounds with antioxidant properties that can help to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation, which may contribute to the alleviation of feverish symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make tinctures for feverish feelings due to their ability to reduce inflammation and calm the nervous system.
Barks: The barks contain bioactive compounds that help to lower body temperature, reducing the severity of feverish symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather organic gelsemium sempervirens root bark and high-proof vodka in equal parts.
Dry the gelsemium sempervirens root bark for at least 24 hours before proceeding.
Combine one part dried gelsemium sempervirens root bark with two parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place and shake the jar daily for four weeks to allow for maceration.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, then store it in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tinctures helps with feverish feeling because of its unique blend of bioactive compounds, particularly capsaicin and beta-carotene. These compounds work synergistically to alleviate symptoms of fever by reducing inflammation, easing congestion, and promoting sweating. The tincture's antimicrobial properties also help combat underlying infections that may be causing the fever, while its antioxidant activity soothes and calms the body. Additionally, bell pepper's cooling effect helps to reduce body temperature, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a sense of well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Capsaicin, found in chili peppers, has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce feverish feeling and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
Analgesic properties: Capasaicin also has analgesic properties, which can help relieve headaches and other pains associated with fever.
Decongestant properties: The capsaicin in chili peppers can help clear nasal passages and sinuses, reducing congestion and making breathing easier when dealing with a cold or sinus infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bell pepper roots are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with fevers such as headaches and body aches.
Buds: Bell pepper buds are thought to contain compounds that may help reduce inflammation and relieve congestion, making them useful in tincture form for feverish feelings.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried capsicum annuum leaves and stems in the morning when dew is present for maximum potency.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka or isopropyl alcohol at a ratio of 1 part herbs to 5 parts solvent.
Transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar with a lid and store it in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks to allow infusion.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids after 20 minutes.
Store the resulting clear liquid capsicum annuum tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid and use within 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with feverish feeling because of its unique properties that help to soothe and calm the body. The active compounds in the herb, such as echinacoside and isobutylamides, have anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects, which can help to reduce swelling and alleviate congestion associated with fever. Additionally, Kansas coneflower's natural antihistamine properties can help to reduce the severity of feverish symptoms like sweating and chills, promoting a sense of relief and relaxation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Kansas Coneflower tincture contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the body and alleviate symptoms associated with fevers such as headache and body aches.
Antimicrobial: The tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections and reduce fever by targeting and eliminating pathogenic microorganisms that cause illness.

Immune system support: Kansas Coneflower contains compounds that have immunomodulatory effects, which means they can help regulate and strengthen the immune system's response to infection, reducing the severity of symptoms and helping the body recover faster from illness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain a high concentration of salicylic acid, which is similar to aspirin and helps to reduce pain and inflammation associated with fever.
Barks: The barks are used because they possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help to combat the underlying causes of fever and alleviate symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh echinacea angustifolia roots and cut them into small pieces for drying.
Dry the root pieces in a single layer on paper towels or wire racks at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Chop the dried roots into smaller fragments and combine with 80 proof vodka in a 1:4 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar with a lid for 2 weeks, shaking daily, to extract the medicinal properties.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discard the solids, and store the tincture in dark glass bottles at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tinctures helps with feverish feeling because of its ability to reduce inflammation and ease congestion. The herb's active compounds, such as achilleic acid and sesquiterpenes, have natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe a sore throat and calm the body's response to infection. As a result, yarrow tinctures can help to break down feverish sweats and alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and sinus infections, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms such as headache, joint pain, and body aches associated with fever.
Antipyretic: Yarrow contains antipyretic properties that help to lower body temperature, providing relief from high fevers and reducing the risk of complications such as dehydration or heatstroke.
Decongestant: Yarrow's decongestant properties can help relieve congestion and sinus pressure associated with fever, making it easier to breathe and promoting overall comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yarrow plant are used to make tinctures as they contain high levels of sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate fever.
Flowers: Yarrow flowers are a common ingredient in fever-reducing tinctures due to their ability to help lower body temperature and ease respiratory congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in the morning.
Dry the harvested herbs using a food dehydrator or air drying method.
Chop the dried achillea millefolium into small pieces to facilitate tincture making.
Steep the chopped herb in a combination of 100 proof vodka or brandy and filtered water.
Allow the mixture to steep for six weeks, shaking the bottle daily to aid extraction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with feverish feeling because they possess anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which work to alleviate the discomfort associated with fevers. The bitter compounds present in dandelion roots have been traditionally used to treat symptoms of fever, such as sweating, chills, and body aches. Additionally, the tannins in dandelion leaves help to reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive system, further aiding in the relief of feverish feelings.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can alleviate symptoms associated with feverish feelings such as body aches and joint pain.

Diuretic and detoxifying properties: Dandelion's diuretic action helps increase urine production, which can help flush out toxins and excess heat from the body, reducing feverish sensations and promoting overall detoxification.

Immune-modulating properties: Dandelion contains compounds that have been shown to stimulate the immune system, helping to reduce the severity of fevers and promote recovery from illnesses by activating natural defenses against infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bitter compounds, which help to reduce fever and alleviate body aches.
Buds: Containing mucilages, which soothe the respiratory tract and relieve congestion associated with fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion leaves and flowers according to seasonal availability and sustainable farming practices.
Rinse the collected plant material with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine one part of fresh dandelion leaves and flowers with two parts of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking daily, to allow for maceration and solvent extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and preserving the resulting tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass tinctures helps with feverish feeling because of its natural ability to cool and calm the body. The citral content in lemongrass has a soothing effect on the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting sweating to help bring down temperature. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of lemongrass tincture help combat underlying infections that may be causing the fever. As the tincture is absorbed into the bloodstream, it helps to ease congestion and discomfort, providing relief from the uncomfortable symptoms associated with feverish feeling.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic properties: Lemongrass tincture has a natural ability to reduce body temperature, helping to alleviate fever symptoms by acting as a mild antiseptic and anti-inflammatory.

Cytokine-modulating activity: Lemongrass contains compounds that can modulate the production of cytokines, which are proteins that help fight off infections, reducing inflammation and alleviating feverish feelings.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in lemongrass tincture help to neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation, further exacerbating fever symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemongrass roots have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of flu and cold due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: The young buds of lemongrass plant are used to make medicinal tinctures, as they contain a high concentration of citral oil, which helps to reduce fever and relieve respiratory congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves in the morning when dew drops are still present for optimal potency.
Clean and chop the harvested leaves into small pieces to facilitate drying and extraction.
Dry the chopped leaves in a warm dark place or using a food dehydrator at 50°f for 24 hours.
Combine the dried cymbopogon citratus leaves with 80% ethanol at a ratio of 1:2 for soaking and maceration for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and store the resulting tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: King of bitters tinctures helps with feverish feeling because it contains a synergistic blend of herbs that work together to calm the body's inflammatory response. The botanicals, such as Gentian and Angelica, have natural antipyretic properties that help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms like sweating, chills, and headaches associated with fever. Additionally, the tincture's bitter compounds stimulate digestion, which can aid in relieving nausea and vomiting often accompanied by fever. By targeting multiple facets of feverish feelings, King of Bitters tinctures provides effective relief and support for the body during this challenging time.
remedy_properties: Antiviral and antibacterial properties: The tincture's combination of herbs, such as goldenseal and echinacea, helps to combat viral and bacterial infections, reducing the severity of fever and promoting recovery.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Bitters like angelica root and gentian help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to feverish feelings, while also alleviating associated symptoms like headache and body aches.
Sweat-inducing properties: The tincture's diaphoretic herbs, such as peppermint and ginger, stimulate sweating, which can help to cool the body and reduce fever by eliminating toxins and excess heat.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Andrographis paniculata contain a high concentration of andrographolide, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, making it effective in reducing fever.
Barks: The barks of the plant are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that can help combat infection and reduce symptoms associated with fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh andrographis paniculata leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried.
Clean and chop 50g of fresh plant material into small pieces using scissors or pruning shears.
Combine chopped plant material with 200ml of vodka at a ratio of 1:5 in a clean glass jar.
Steep mixture for 14 days in a cool dark place shaking the jar twice daily to extract tannins and flavonoids.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tinctures helps with feverish feeling because it contains a unique combination of essential oils, including eucalyptus and menthol, which work together to ease congestion and reduce inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties of Tasmanian blue gum help to bring down the body's temperature, providing relief from feverish sensations. Additionally, its decongestant properties help to clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating the feeling of heat and discomfort associated with a fever.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic property: Tasmanian Blue Gum tincture helps reduce body temperature by relaxing blood vessels and increasing sweating, which in turn cools the body down.

Anti-inflammatory property: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as eucalyptol, help reduce swelling and ease congestion, making it an effective remedy for relieving symptoms of feverish feeling.

Expectorant property: Eucalyptus globulus' expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, allowing for easier breathing and alleviating coughs and chest congestion often associated with fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing fever symptoms.
Barks: Contains a high concentration of salicylic acid, similar to aspirin, which can help alleviate headaches, sore throats, and other symptoms associated with fevers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves at dawn or early morning when essential oils are most concentrated for highest potency.
Dry the freshly harvested leaves in a warm, dry, dark place for 7-10 days to remove excess moisture and preserve quality.
Combine one part dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with two parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar for every 8 ounces of liquid.
Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily, to allow for extraction and maturation of tincture.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding solids, and store in a cool, dark place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with feverish feeling because of its natural cooling and soothing properties. The herb contains compounds like rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms associated with fevers. Additionally, lemon balm's calming effects can help to ease feelings of restlessness and irritability that often accompany feverish states, promoting a sense of relaxation and relief.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic properties: Lemon balm's antipyretic properties help to reduce fever by inhibiting the release of inflammatory compounds and decreasing body temperature.
Anxiolytic properties: Lemon balm's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties can help alleviate feelings of anxiety and restlessness often associated with fevers, promoting relaxation and calmness.
Antiviral properties: Lemon balm's antiviral properties may help to combat the underlying viral cause of fever, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, lemon balm roots are known for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in reducing fever.
Buds: Lemon balm buds contain high levels of citral, a natural antihistamine that can help alleviate symptoms of feverish feeling such as headaches and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves from your garden or purchase them from a reputable supplier weighing approximately 500g.
Wash the leaves gently with cold water to remove any dirt or debris and pat dry with a clean cloth.
Combine the dried melissa officinalis leaves with vodka in a glass jar at a ratio of 1 part herb to 5 parts solvent steeping for 6 weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container discarding the solids and retaining the liquid mixture.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place allowing it to mature for an additional 2 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with feverish feeling because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that soothe a sore throat, reducing discomfort and congestion. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has natural antiviral and antimicrobial properties, which help combat infections causing fevers. Additionally, its demulcent properties create a protective barrier in the digestive tract, easing any stomach upset often associated with feverishness. By addressing multiple aspects of a fever, herbal licorice tinctures can provide relief from the discomfort and distress caused by elevated temperatures.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Licorice root tincture contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against various viruses, including herpes simplex and influenza, helping to combat the underlying cause of fever.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tannins and flavonoids present in licorice root tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and pain associated with fever, while also soothing the mucous membranes to alleviate discomfort.

Adaptogenic properties: Licorice root tincture is believed to exhibit adaptogenic effects, helping the body cope with stress and promoting balance within the body's physiological systems, which can help regulate body temperature and alleviate symptoms of fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant have been traditionally used to treat feverish feelings due to their anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms associated with fever.

Rhyzomes: Licorice rhyzomes contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to exhibit antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in reducing the severity of feverish feelings.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in sufficient quantity for the desired yield of tincture.
Clean and chop the harvested roots into small pieces to facilitate extraction.
Combine one part chopped root material with two parts 95% ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every other day to ensure proper extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container, discarding the solids and storing the liquid in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed tinctures helps with feverish feeling because its active compounds, particularly nepetalactone, have a natural anti-inflammatory and antipyretic effect. This allows it to effectively reduce fever and alleviate associated symptoms such as chills, sweating, and discomfort. Additionally, the herb's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and relieve congestion. remedy_properties: Tannins: Tannins in Joe Pye Weed tincture help to reduce inflammation and soothe a sore throat, which can be particularly beneficial during feverous illnesses.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in the herb have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making them effective against viral infections that often cause feverish feelings.

Essential Oils: The essential oils found in Joe Pye Weed tincture, including camphor, limonene, and pinene, possess decongestant and expectorant properties, which can help to relieve congestion and open up airways, alleviating symptoms of fever and cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Joe Pye Weed contain a high concentration of ecdysteroid, which has been traditionally used to reduce fever and alleviate flu-like symptoms.
Barks: The bark of the Joe Pye Weed plant contains a compound called pyridine, which is believed to have antipyretic properties, helping to cool the body and reduce fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried eupatorium perfoliatum leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Combine one part of eupatorium perfoliatum with one part of vodka or pure grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids after 30 minutes.
Label and store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, ready for use within one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Barberry tinctures helps with feverish feeling because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce body heat and alleviate symptoms of fever. The berberine present in barberry is known for its antipyretic effects, which can help to bring down a high temperature quickly and safely. remedy_properties: Berberine: Barberry tinctures contain berberine, a natural antibacterial agent that helps to reduce inflammation and combat infections that can cause feverish feelings.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe the body and reduce swelling, which can contribute to relief from feverish discomfort.
Antioxidant activity: Barberry tinctures possess antioxidant properties that help protect the body from oxidative stress, which can exacerbate fevers and other inflammatory responses.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids and glycosides, roots are a common source of berberine, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties.
Bark: The bark of the barberry plant contains high concentrations of berberine, making it a popular choice for creating medicinal tinctures that target feverish feelings.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh barberry roots and stems, ensuring a minimum of 20% berberine content.
Dry the harvested material in a warm place with air circulation for at least 7 days.
Grind 10 grams of dried berberis vulgaris into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar.
Steep the powdered plant material in 50 milliliters of 100 proof vodka for 2 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain and filter the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter paper to remove solids and achieve a 1:3 ratio.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod tinctures helps with feverish feeling because it contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is often a symptom of fever. Additionally, goldenrod's antipyretic properties make it effective in lowering elevated body temperatures, providing relief from feverish discomfort. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Goldenrod's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and ease congestion, alleviating fever-related discomforts like headache and body ache.
Antimicrobial activity: Goldenrod's antimicrobial properties help combat the underlying infection or pathogen causing the fever, reducing its severity and duration.

Expectorant properties: Goldenrod's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and alleviating coughs and congestion associated with fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the goldenrod plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing fever and alleviating associated symptoms.
Barks: Goldenrod barks are rich in flavonoids, which possess antiviral and antibacterial properties, helping to combat infections that often cause feverish feelings.
remedy_recipe: Harvest solidago virgaurea flowers and leaves in mid-summer for optimal potency and quality.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator or air circulation method within 24 hours.
Grind 50 grams of dried solidago virgaurea into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground herb with 200 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a glass jar, then seal and store for four weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discarding solids, and transfer to dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with feverish feeling because of their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The elder plant, Sambucus nigra, has been traditionally used for centuries to combat viral infections and reduce fever. When taken as a tincture, the bioactive compounds in elder root and leaves work quickly to calm the body's inflammatory response, helping to alleviate symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, and sweating that often accompany feverish feelings. remedy_properties: Antiviral and antiseptic properties: Elder tincture's ability to combat viruses and bacteria helps reduce fever by eliminating the underlying infection, thus alleviating symptoms.

Sudorific and diaphoretic properties: The tincture's ability to stimulate sweating helps bring out toxins through perspiration, which can aid in reducing body temperature and relieving discomfort associated with fever.

Anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties: Elder tincture's anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce the severity of inflammation caused by fever, while its antipyretic properties can directly help lower body temperature.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots are valued for their antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing fever and alleviating associated symptoms.
Barks: The bark of the elder tree contains compounds that help to cool and calm the body, making it a natural remedy for fevers and hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh or dried sambucus nigra leaves and flowers.
Combine the herbs with 250ml of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for at least 6 weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store it in the refrigerator for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with feverish feeling because its natural properties have a calming effect on the body, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Expectorant properties: Fennel tincture's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, relieving congestion and coughs often associated with fevers.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems, selecting only those with no signs of disease or pest infestation.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces, aiming for a total weight of one pound.
Combine the chopped foeniculum vulgare with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring a 1:5 ratio by volume.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for eight to ten weeks, shaking the jar once daily during that time period.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids and labeling the resulting liquid tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with feverish feeling because its sedative properties have a profound impact on the body's response to heat. The tincture's active ingredients, such as valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, work to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of anxiety and restlessness that often accompany fevers. Additionally, valerian's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe the body's internal temperature regulatory systems, promoting a sense of coolness and relief from discomfort. As a result, herbal valerian tinctures can provide natural support for those struggling with feverish feelings.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian tincture's sedative properties can help alleviate symptoms of fever such as restlessness, agitation, and insomnia, promoting a sense of calmness and relaxation.
Antispasmodic activity: Valerian's antispasmodic properties can help ease muscle spasms and cramping that often accompany fevers, reducing discomfort and promoting relaxation.

Inflammation reduction: Valerian contains flavonoids and volatile oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation associated with feverish feelings and alleviate symptoms such as headaches and joint pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are commonly used in herbal medicine because they contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have sedative and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate feverish symptoms.

Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes, the underground stems that connect the roots to the rest of the plant, are also used in herbal medicine due to their ability to produce a calming effect on the nervous system, making them effective in treating fever-induced anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots and cut into small pieces weighing approximately 2 cups.
Combine the chopped root material with 100 proof vodka filling a clean glass jar to the top leaving no headspace.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for at least four weeks shaking it daily during this preparation time.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Transfer the final tincture to small amber glass bottles sealing them tightly and storing them in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with feverish feeling because of its natural cooling and calming properties. The menthol content in peppermint oil is absorbed quickly into the bloodstream, providing a soothing and relaxing sensation that can help to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms such as sweating, chills, and headaches associated with fever. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce swelling and ease congestion, further aiding in the relief of feverish discomfort.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's cooling and analgesic properties help to relieve headaches, fever chills, and body aches associated with fever, providing rapid and effective relief from discomfort.

Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol's decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties help to ease congestion, open airways, and reduce mucus production, making it easier to breathe and alleviating the stuffy feeling that often accompanies a fever.

Caryophyllene: Caryophyllene's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce pain, swelling, and inflammation in the body, which can be particularly helpful when experiencing fever-related discomfort such as sore throats, muscles, or joints.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint are used to make tinctures due to their high concentration of menthol, which helps to cool and soothe the body, reducing fever.
Buds: Peppermint buds contain a higher concentration of menthol than older leaves, making them an ideal choice for creating tinctures that target feverish feelings.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or local market weighing at least 500 grams.
Chop the peppermint leaves into smaller pieces to ensure uniform extraction and increase surface area.
Combine the chopped peppermint leaves with 95% ethyl alcohol in a ratio of 1:4 by weight steeping for four weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids after 24 hours.
Transfer the tincture to dark amber glass bottles storing it in a cool dark place for at least six months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose tinctures helps with feverish feeling because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa rugosa petals and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated ensuring optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours or air-drying.
Combine one part dried rosa rugosa with three parts vodka in a clean glass jar filling to the top ensuring maximum exposure.
Store the jar in a cool dark place shaking daily for 2-4 weeks allowing for extraction and maturation.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding solids and storing the tincture in dark glass bottles.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality goldenseal root, ensuring it's free from contamination and pests.
Dry the harvested goldenseal roots in a cool, dark place for at least two weeks.
Chop the dried goldenseal roots into small pieces to increase surface area and facilitate extraction.
Combine 10 parts of chopped goldenseal with 40 parts of high-proof vodka in a glass jar and seal tightly.
Allow the mixture to steep for six weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining and filtering the tincture.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers and chop them into small pieces using scissors.
Combine one part chopped calendula flowers with three parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 to 8 weeks, shaking the jar daily for 15 minutes.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat feverish feeling with herbal tinctures is to combine Echinacea, Goldenseal, and Yarrow in a 1/2 teaspoon dose, three times daily. Additionally, drink plenty of fluids to help the body replenish lost electrolytes. Consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies, especially if you have a weakened immune system or are taking prescription medications.
